title: Important Dates for IIT Roorkee PhD Admission 2026

detail.title: Important Dates for IIT Roorkee PhD Admission 2026
detail.short_info: IIT Roorkee PhD Admission 2026 (Autumn Semester 2026-27) application is open from 24 March to 20 April 2026. Check eligibility, departments, application fee, selection process, and direct links for doctoral programmes at the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee.

content.text: IIT Roorkee PhD Admission 2026 (Autumn Semester 2026-27) is now open at the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee (IIT Roorkee / IITR). Online applications started on 24 March 2026, and the last date to submit the form is 20 April 2026. Selected candidates will complete in-person academic registration on 13-14 July 2026, with classes commencing from 16 July 2026.

This guide provides complete and accurate details on eligibility, departments, application process, selection procedure, fees, and important links to help serious PhD aspirants apply successfully for doctoral programmes at one of India’s top-ranked technical institutes.
Direct Link to Download IIT Roorkee PhD Admission 2026 - Click Here

sections.heading: Important Dates for IIT Roorkee PhD Admission 2026
Eligibility Criteria for IIT Roorkee PhD 2026

content.text: Candidates must satisfy the Common Minimum Eligibility Criteria (CMEC) and the department-specific Minimum Educational Qualification (MEQ).
Common Minimum Eligibility Criteria (CMEC):

items: Master’s degree holders: Minimum CGPA 6.0 (60%) for GEN/GEN-EWS/OBC and 5.5 (55%) for SC/ST/PwD.
Must have qualified a national level test (GATE, CEED, JEST, UGC-NET, CSIR-NET, or equivalent) or hold a national fellowship (in most cases).
Exemptions:
B.Tech/M.Sc with CGPA ≥8.0 from MoE-funded institutions (CFTIs).
Bachelor’s/Master’s from IITs with CGPA ≥8.0.
4-year Bachelor’s with CGPA ≥7.5 + national test (non-CFTI).
MBBS/BAMS/BHMS/BDS with CGPA 6.0.
MS/MD degrees (exempt from CGPA and test).

content.text: Department-wise MEQ details (Engineering, Sciences, Architecture, Management, Humanities, Nanotechnology, Data Science, etc.) are given in the official Information Brochure.

sections.heading: Departments Offering PhD at IIT Roorkee

content.text: IIT Roorkee offers PhD programmes on both Roorkee and Saharanpur campuses across a wide range of disciplines, including:

Architecture & Planning, Biosciences and Bioengineering, Civil Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Chemistry, Computer Science & Engineering, Electronics & Communication Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical & Industrial Engineering, Metallurgical & Materials Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, Humanities & Social Sciences, Management Studies, Hydrology, Hydro & Renewable Energy, Water Resources Development & Management, Centre for Nanotechnology, Centre for Disaster Mitigation & Management, Mehta Family School of Data Science and Artificial Intelligence, and many more interdisciplinary centres.
For the complete list and specific eligibility, refer to the official brochure.

sections.heading: Selection Process for IIT Roorkee PhD Admission

content.text: Admission is purely merit-based. Shortlisted candidates will be called for a written test and/or interview conducted by the respective Department/Centre/School. Departments will intimate the dates directly to shortlisted applicants and upload them on their portals. Final selection depends on academic record, test/interview performance, and availability of seats/supervisors.

sections.heading: How to Apply for IIT Roorkee PhD Admission 2026?

items: Visit the official IIT Roorkee admission portal.
Read the Ph.D. Information Brochure (Autumn 2026) carefully.
Register and fill the online application form.
Upload required documents (photo, signature, marksheets, category certificate, GATE/NET scorecard if applicable, etc.).
Pay the application fee online.
Submit the form and note the application number for future reference.

content.text: Apply Online Link: Available on the official IIT Roorkee website.
Application Fee:

items: ₹500 per Department/Centre/School for GEN/GEN-EWS/OBC-NCL candidates.
₹250 per Department/Centre/School for SC/ST/PwD candidates.

content.text: Fee is non-refundable and must be paid online.

items: Full-Time: Institute Assistantship Research Scholars (IARS), Externally Funded (EFRS), Project-funded, Foreign Nationals.
Part-Time: In-Service Internal (P-ISIRS), In-Service External (P-ISERS / ISERS).
Special Schemes: Golden Girl Scheme and SAKUNTALA Fellowship for meritorious female candidates (direct interview, fellowship benefits).
